data.title: Lexeo Therapeutics names Kyle Rasbach as CFO
data.description:
• Lexeo Therapeutics (NASDAQ:LXEO) said it has appointed Kyle Rasbach as its finance chief, effective immediately.
  • Most recently, Rasbach was the chief business officer at Zentalis Pharmaceuticals.

data.title: Climb Bio set to join Nasdaq Biotech Index
data.description:
• Climb Bio (NASDAQ:CLYM) said it will be added to the Nasdaq Biotech Index.
  • The inclusion will be effective prior to market open on December 23.

data.title: Darden Restaurants Non-GAAP EPS of $2.03 beats by $0.01, revenue of $2.89B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Darden Restaurants press release (NYSE:DRI): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.03 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $2.89B (+5.9% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • Total sales increased 6.0% to $2.9 billion, driven by a blended same-restaurant sales* increase of 2.4% and sales from the addition of 103 Chuy's restaurants and 39 net new restaurants
    • Same-restaurant sales:

    Consolidated Darden*

    2.4 %

    Olive Garden

    2.0 %

    LongHorn Steakhouse

    7.5 %

    Fine Dining**

    (5.8) %

    Other Business*

    0.7 %
  • Fiscal 2025 Financial Outlook
  • Total sales of approximately $12.1 billion
  • Same-restaurant sales growth of approximately 1.5%*
  • New restaurant openings of 50 to 55
  • Total capital spending of approximately $650 million
  • Total inflation of approximately 2.5%
  • An effective tax rate of approximately 12.5%
  • Diluted net earnings per share from continuing operations of $9.40 to $9.60, excluding approximately $47 million, pre-tax, of Chuy's transaction and integration related costs**
  • Approximately 118 million weighted average diluted shares outstanding

data.title: Bank of England holds interest rates steady at 4.75% as price pressure lingers
data.description:
The Bank of England has voted against a further cut to interest rates, after the latest annual UK inflation figures hit 8-month high.

The nine rate-setters on the Bank’s Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) voted 6-3 to keep the base rate unchanged at 4.75 per cent.

The annual inflation rate in the UK edged up for a second month to 2.6% in November 2024 from 2.3% in October, matching forecasts. It is the highest inflation rate in eight months.

The decision comes a day after the US Federal Reserve voted for a 0.25 per cent cut to US interest rates to 4.25% to 4.50%, marking the third rate cut this year.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB)

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Accenture reports mixed FQ1 results; initiates FQ2, raises FY revenue outlook
Nordstrom family, El Puerto de Liverpool near deal for Nordstrom - report
data.description:
The Nordstrom family and Mexican department store chain El Puerto de Liverpool (OTCPK:ELPQF) are close to signing a deal to purchase Nordstrom Inc. (NYSE:JWN).

An agreement could be reached this week or if not before the end of the year, according to a Women's Wear Daily report from late Wednesday.

Nordstrom didn't immediately respond to WWD request for comment.

The Nordstrom family offered to buy the famed department store chain for $3.8 billion, or $23 a share in September.

Mexican department store chain Liverpool took an almost 10% stake in Nordstrom (JWN) two years.

The September offer came after Nordstrom (JWN) in April confirmed that its board formed a special committee of independent directors after two members of the founding family expressed interest in a possible take private. Nordstrom (JWN) rejected a takeover offer from its founding family in 2018 when the family offered $50 a share.

data.title: Lamb Weston falls 19% after slashing guidance due to soft volume trends
data.description:
Lamb Weston Holdings (NYSE:LW) fell sharply on Thursday after falling short of estimates with its FQ3 report and setting guidance below expectations.

CEO Tom Werner said higher-than-expected manufacturing costs and softer volumes accounted for the earnings shortfall.

Sales declined 8% year-over-year to $131.2 million. Volume declined 6%, largely reflecting the impact of soft global restaurant traffic trends, customer share losses, and the carryover effect of the company's decision in to exit certain lower-priced and lower-margin business in Europe. Price/mix declined 2% in FQ2, reflecting the impact of planned investments in price and trade support to attract and retain volume in North America, pricing actions in key international markets in response to a more competitive environment, and unfavorable channel and product mix.

Adjusted gross profit declined by more than 25% to $343.5 million due to unfavorable price/mix, higher manufacturing costs per pound, and lower sales volumes. EPS was reported at $0.66 vs. $1.01 consensus and $1.45 a year ago.

Looking ahead, Lamb Weston (LW) expects challenging conditions to persist through the remainder of fiscal 2025 and into fiscal 2026, driven primarily by an accelerating rate of capacity additions and continued near-term softening of global frozen potato demand below historical rates, particularly outside North America, until demand trends improve and capacity expansion normalizes.

Lamb Weston (LW) guided for FY25 revenue of $6.35 billion to $6.45 billion (midpoint $6.40 billion) vs. $6.66 billion consensus and EPS of $3.05 to $3.20 (midpoint $3.125) vs. a prior outlook for $4.15 to $4.35 and $4.21 consensus.

Shares of Lamb Weston (LW) were down 18.18% in premarket trading. Last week, shares rose on a report that Post Holdings is exploring a deal with the company.

data.title: VEON completes planned move of headquarters to Dubai
data.description:
• VEON (NASDAQ:VEON) announced on Thursday the completion of the planned move of its headquarters from Amsterdam to Dubai, United Arab Emirates.
  • The company had announced the plan in October.
  • The move makes VEON the largest Nasdaq-listed company with headquarters in Dubai.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Accenture declares $1.48 dividend

data.title: Vertex’s non-opioid drug succeeds in back pain trial
data.description:
Vertex Pharmaceuticals’ (NASDAQ:VRTX) investigational non-opioid drug, suzetrigine, has met the primary endpoint in a mid-stage study in people with painful lumbosacral radiculopathy (LSR).

Suzetrigine, an oral, highly selective NaV1.8 pain signal inhibitor, showed statistically significant and clinically meaningful reduction in pain on the numeric pain rating scale (NPRS) in the Phase 2 study, with a mean change in pain score at week 12 of -2.02.

This 11-point scale ranges from 0 (no pain) to 10 (worst pain imaginable). However, VRTX shares tumbled ~13% premarket as the biotech said a placebo arm showed a similar reduction (-1.98) in pain scores as suzetrigine.

Suzetrigine was generally well tolerated in the study. The incidence of adverse events was 22.9% in the suzetrigine arm and 32.4% in the placebo arm.

Vertex (VRTX) plans to advance suzetrigine into pivotal development for painful LSR following discussions with regulators on the study design and regulatory package.

The company stressed that its approach is to selectively inhibit NaV1.8 using small molecules with the objective of creating a new class of pain signal inhibitors that have the potential to provide effective relief of pain without the limitations of currently available therapies, including the addictive potential of opioids.

Lumbosacral radiculopathy is a neurologic condition that occurs when a nerve root in the lower back is compressed or irritated. Millions of patients suffer from pain due to LSR every year.
